pattern: Nuxt Font module with Inter as SF Pro fallback
implementation: // nuxt.config.ts: modules: ['@nuxt/fonts']; fonts: { families: [{ name: 'Inter' provider: 'google' }] }; // app.vue: <style> body { font-family: 'Inter' -apple-system BlinkMacSystemFont system-ui sans-serif; } </style>
anti_pattern: Loading fonts via <link> tag in public HTML or @import in CSS
notes: @nuxt/fonts self-hosts and optimizes Inter eliminating layout shift matching Apple's load quality

pattern: app.vue layout for consistent Apple chrome
anti_pattern: Repeating navbar and footer markup in every page component
notes: Single app.vue shell persists navigation between routes without remounting

pattern: useColorMode() composable for dark mode
implementation: // nuxt.config.ts: modules: ['@nuxtjs/color-mode']; colorMode: { classSuffix: '' preference: 'system' fallback: 'light' }; // Component: const colorMode = useColorMode(); const isDark = computed(() => colorMode.value === 'dark');
anti_pattern: JavaScript-only dark mode toggle ignoring system preference
notes: @nuxtjs/color-mode respects prefers-color-scheme and persists preference without flash

pattern: NuxtLink with Apple-style transitions
implementation: <!-- nuxt.config.ts --> app: { pageTransition: { name: 'page' mode: 'out-in' } }; /* app.css */ .page-enter-active .page-leave-active { transition: opacity 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.25 0.1 0.25 1) transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.25 0.1 0.25 1); } .page-enter-from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); } .page-leave-to { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-8px); }
anti_pattern: Hard navigation without transitions or jarring instant page swaps
notes: Apple-caliber page transitions make the app feel continuous rather than a collection of pages

pattern: definePageMeta for per-page layout options
implementation: <!-- pages/landing.vue --> <script setup> definePageMeta({ layout: 'marketing' transition: { name: 'slide-left' mode: 'out-in' } }) </script>
anti_pattern: Manually importing and wrapping layout components per page
notes: definePageMeta is the Nuxt 3 idiomatic way to opt into layouts and transitions per route

pattern: useHead for Apple-style metadata
implementation: useHead({ title: 'Product – Brand' meta: [ { name: 'description' content: 'Description' } { property: 'og:image' content: '/og.jpg' } { name: 'viewport' content: 'width=device-width initial-scale=1 viewport-fit=cover' } { name: 'apple-mobile-web-app-capable' content: 'yes' } ] link: [{ rel: 'apple-touch-icon' href: '/apple-touch-icon.png' }] });
anti_pattern: Manual <head> tags scattered across page templates
notes: useHead is reactive and SSR-safe; viewport-fit=cover enables edge-to-edge iOS web app mode

pattern: Nuxt server routes for lightweight API
implementation: // server/api/products.get.ts export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => { const products = await fetchProducts(); return products; }); // Usage: const { data } = await useFetch('/api/products');
anti_pattern: External Express server or heavy API framework for simple endpoints
notes: Server routes collocate API with the Nuxt app; zero extra deployment matching Apple's tight integration

pattern: Nuxt Image module for optimized hero images
implementation: // nuxt.config.ts: modules: ['@nuxt/image']; // Template: <NuxtImg src='/hero.jpg' alt='Hero' width='1200' height='600' format='webp' placeholder loading='eager' fetchpriority='high' />
anti_pattern: Static src without WebP conversion or responsive sizes
notes: @nuxt/image generates WebP srcset and blur placeholder matching Apple.com's progressive loading

pattern: CSS custom properties in assets/css/main.css
implementation: /* assets/css/main.css */ :root { --color-text-primary: #1d1d1f; --color-text-secondary: #86868b; --color-bg: #fbfbfd; --color-blue: #2997ff; --space-xs: 4px; --space-sm: 8px; --space-md: 16px; --space-lg: 24px; --space-xl: 48px; } [data-color-mode='dark'] { --color-bg: #000000; --color-text-primary: #f5f5f7; }
anti_pattern: Tailwind arbitrary values or per-component hardcoded colors
notes: Single token file ensures all Apple color and spacing values are overridable with one variable

pattern: Tailwind CSS with Apple design token preset
implementation: // tailwind.config.ts: theme: { extend: { colors: { 'apple-blue': '#2997ff' 'apple-text': '#1d1d1f' 'apple-secondary': '#86868b' 'apple-bg': '#fbfbfd' } spacing: { 'section': '80px' 'section-lg': '120px' } borderRadius: { 'apple': '12px' 'apple-lg': '18px' } } }
anti_pattern: Default Tailwind palette without Apple color customization
notes: Extending Tailwind with Apple tokens ensures utility classes produce HIG-compliant output

pattern: Backdrop-filter in TheNavbar.vue component
implementation: <template> <nav class='navbar'> <slot /> </nav> </template> <style scoped> .navbar { backdrop-filter: saturate(180%) blur(20px); background: rgba(255 255 255 / 0.72); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0 0 0 / 0.1); position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100; } </style>
anti_pattern: Opaque background on sticky navigation bar
notes: Apple's frosted glass nav is its most recognizable cross-platform design signature

pattern: NuxtPage transitions with spring-like curves
implementation: /* assets/css/transitions.css */ .page-enter-active { transition: transform 0.45s cubic-bezier(0.25 0.46 0.45 0.94) opacity 0.35s ease; } .page-leave-active { transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.55 0 1 0.45) opacity 0.25s ease; }
anti_pattern: Default Vue fade transition or no page transition at all
notes: Spring-like asymmetric enter/leave curves match the feel of UIKit navigation push/pop

pattern: useFetch with skeleton loading states
anti_pattern: Showing spinner or blank page during data fetch
notes: Content silhouettes at correct size prevent layout shift matching Apple's iOS skeleton pattern

pattern: Nuxt UI components customized for Apple style
implementation: // nuxt.config.ts if using @nuxt/ui: ui: { primary: 'blue' gray: 'neutral' button: { rounded: 'rounded-xl' } card: { rounded: 'rounded-2xl' shadow: 'shadow-sm' } }
anti_pattern: Default Nuxt UI styling without Apple-aligned customization
notes: @nuxt/ui's headless primitives can be tuned to Apple proportions reducing custom CSS

pattern: error.vue with clean Apple error design
anti_pattern: Exposing raw stack traces or using default browser error page
notes: Clean minimal error pages match Apple's principle of calm humane technology

pattern: defineNuxtPlugin for global Apple theme initialization
implementation: // plugins/apple-theme.client.ts export default defineNuxtPlugin(() => { const root = document.documentElement; root.style.setProperty('--color-blue' '#2997ff'); // Set initial theme from OS const isDark = matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches; root.setAttribute('data-color-mode' isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'); });
anti_pattern: Initializing theme in onMounted of individual components
notes: Client-only plugin runs once before app mounts preventing flash of wrong theme

pattern: Middleware for route guards with Apple-style redirects
implementation: // middleware/auth.ts export default defineNuxtRouteMiddleware((to) => { const { loggedIn } = useAuth(); if (!loggedIn.value && to.path !== '/login') { return navigateTo('/login'); } });
anti_pattern: Client-side router guards in component onMounted
notes: Nuxt middleware runs server-side preventing unauthorized page flash before redirect

pattern: prefers-color-scheme in nuxt.config.ts colorMode
implementation: // nuxt.config.ts colorMode: { preference: 'system' fallback: 'light' classSuffix: '' storageKey: 'color-mode' }
anti_pattern: Defaulting to light mode and ignoring system preference
notes: System preference as default respects user's OS-level accessibility and battery settings

pattern: Apple spacing scale in tailwind.config.ts
anti_pattern: Default Tailwind spacing scale without Apple grid alignment
notes: Apple's 4pt-base grid maps cleanly to these spacing values for pixel-perfect HIG layouts

pattern: Section components with generous vertical padding
anti_pattern: Tight sections with small vertical padding or full-bleed text
notes: Apple uses 80–120px vertical breathing room between major content sections as core design language
